user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear of the car is clearly visible from an angle that allows assessment of deformation and surface damage on the rear bumper/body area.
claim_status_justification: The image shows clear rear-end deformation with a dented/crumpled rear bumper and trunk area, matching the claim that the back of the car has new damage, mostly in the rear bumper area.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The front of the car and front bumper area are visible in the provided images, allowing assessment of surface marks and deformation.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 shows visible scrape/scratch damage on the front bumper area near the right front corner. Image 2 shows the car front but does not contradict the damage claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The windshield is clearly visible in both images and the damage can be inspected.
claim_status_justification: Both images show a car windshield with visible cracking/spidering consistent with impact damage. The claimed front glass damage is present in the images. [Downgraded: model said supported but cited no images.]

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: A car is visible, and the side mirror is clearly shown closely enough to inspect its condition.
claim_status_justification: The visible side mirror on the car shows clear cracking/shattering damage, consistent with the claim that the mirror was damaged and not sitting correctly.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear of the car and rear bumper are visible clearly enough to inspect for damage, and there are two usable images.
claim_status_justification: The images show visible deformation/denting on the rear bumper area, consistent with the claim that the back bumper was damaged after being tapped from behind. [Downgraded: model said supported but cited no images.]

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The car is visible, but the claimed headlight is not visible clearly enough to inspect for cracks or breakage.
claim_status_justification: A car is present, but the headlight area is not shown clearly enough to determine whether it is cracked or damaged.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Only one image shows a car clearly enough to assess damage, and it shows a dent on the side door area. The other image is too blurry to evaluate. Minimum evidence requires the claimed panel be visible from an angle where surface deformation can be assessed; this is only partially met because there is just one usable view.
claim_status_justification: The clear image shows a visible dent on the side door panel area, matching the claim. The second image is unusable due to blur, but the first image provides direct evidence of the claimed damage.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image shows a car, but the claimed hood surface is not visible in a usable way because the hood is open and the view is dominated by the engine bay and front-end damage. Surface scratch assessment on the hood cannot be made from this angle.
claim_status_justification: A car is present, but the claimed hood scratch is not clearly visible or assessable in the provided image. The image does not provide enough evidence to confirm or deny a scratch on the hood.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The provided image clearly shows an open laptop with the screen visible enough to inspect for cracks.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a laptop with a visibly shattered/cracked display glass, matching the claim that the screen has a crack.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: A laptop is visible, but the claimed hinge damage is not clearly inspectable in the provided images. Image img_1 is a close-up of the hinge/lid corner area with visible wear/damage, but img_2 is too distant to inspect the hinge mechanism or confirm wobble. The minimum evidence standard for this claim family requires the laptop screen, keyboard, or trackpad area to be visible clearly enough to inspect damage; the available images do not provide clear, usable evidence of the claimed mechanical hinge failure.
claim_status_justification: The images show a laptop, but they do not clearly demonstrate a broken hinge or screen wobble. There is visible wear/damage near the hinge area in the close-up, yet the actual mechanical failure claimed is not confirmed from the photos alone.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop keyboard area is clearly visible and can be inspected for liquid-related staining or surface damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a laptop keyboard area covered with liquid droplets and pooled water, consistent with the claimed spill affecting the keys/keyboard area. No display damage is visible, matching the claim transcript.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image shows a laptop lid/corner with enough context to identify the relevant part, and the close-up image provides usable evidence of the claimed corner damage.
claim_status_justification: The images show a laptop and a visible dent/impact mark near an outer corner on the lid/body edge. This matches the claim that one corner is damaged after a fall. [Downgraded: model said supported but cited no images.]

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop screen is clearly visible enough to inspect for cracks, and the damage pattern is discernible despite mild blur.
claim_status_justification: The provided image shows a laptop with a visibly cracked/shattered screen, matching the claim of screen damage.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The laptop is visible and the trackpad/front palm-rest area is shown clearly enough to inspect surface damage, despite some blur.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a laptop with a visible light scratch/mark in the front area near the trackpad/palm-rest edge, consistent with the claimed physical damage around the trackpad area.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The package exterior and the claimed corner are clearly visible enough to inspect for damage.
claim_status_justification: The image shows a package box with a visibly crushed and creased corner at the top edge, matching the claim that one corner was crushed in.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show a package exterior, and image img_1 clearly shows the claimed packaging area with visible tearing and ripped tape on the side/seal region.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows a cardboard package with a visibly torn and ripped taped seam on the side/seal area, matching the claim that the seal side was torn/opened-looking. Image img_2 shows an intact package and does not negate the damage visible in img_1.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The package surface is visible clearly enough to assess exterior staining/water damage. Although the image is somewhat blurry, the dark wet-looking stain on the box face is still discernible.
claim_status_justification: The provided image shows a package with a prominent dark wet-looking stain on the exterior surface, matching the claim that the package looked water damaged.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows an opened package with the contents area visible; img_2 shows a sealed box but is not needed for the missing-contents assessment.
claim_status_justification: The visible opened box in img_1 is filled with crumpled packing material/newspaper, so the contents area is not empty. The claim that the ordered item was missing from the package is not supported by the images.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The provided image shows a cylindrical food container/can, not a shipping package or box exterior. The claimed package object is not visible, so the minimum packaging evidence standard is not met.
claim_status_justification: No shipping box or package is visible in the image. The image instead shows a can/container, so the claim about a crushed outside box is contradicted by the evidence.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the exterior of a package and the seal/tape area, which is sufficient to inspect for torn-open packaging damage.
claim_status_justification: The images show a package with visibly torn/open seal tape and damaged cardboard at the seal area, matching the claim that the package arrived opened and the seal area looked torn. [Downgraded: model said supported but cited no images.]
